products.name: AXX Platform Strategy
products.descriptor: IC engine and hybrid propulsion platform architecture covering N, P, and S powertrain configurations

evidence.snippet: **AXX****N** – Standard IC engine platform. Shaft power is motive (connected directly to the propeller or pump). Generator provides enough electrical power to run comms, avionics, sensors, etc.
**AXX****S** – Full series hybrid whereby all available engine shaft power is converted to electrical energy. Use cases include electrically driven craft and gen-sets.

products.notes: AXX naming: N=standard IC shaft drive, P=parallel hybrid, S=series hybrid/genset. Applied to both A33 and A99 families.

products.name: A33 Propulsion Systems
products.descriptor: 33cc-class IC engine propulsion family for UAVs, with N, HF, and S variants

products.name: A33N
products.descriptor: Standard IC engine UAV propulsion unit; shaft power drives propeller or pump directly

evidence.snippet: **AXX****N** – Standard IC engine platform. Shaft power is motive (connected directly to the propeller or pump). Generator provides enough electrical power to run comms, avionics, sensors, etc.

products.name: A33HF
products.descriptor: Parallel hybrid variant of A33 engine; motor/generator augments shaft power and increases generator output

evidence.snippet: **AXX****P** – Parallel hybrid. Motor/generator can assist the IC engine in providing additional shaft power for time-limited requirements (sprint, take-off, etc.). Additionally, this topology provides more generator output than the ‘N’ configuration.

products.name: A33S
products.descriptor: Series hybrid A33 variant converting all engine shaft power to electrical energy for gen-set or electric propulsion use

evidence.snippet: **AXX****S** – Full series hybrid whereby all available engine shaft power is converted to electrical energy. Use cases include electrically driven craft and gen-sets.

products.notes: Listed on the platform-strategy page without a dedicated hyperlink (unlike A33N and A33HF), suggesting it may be in development or available on inquiry only.

products.name: A99 Propulsion Systems
products.descriptor: 99cc-class IC engine propulsion family for UAVs, with H and S hybrid variants

products.name: A99H
products.descriptor: Parallel hybrid variant of A99 engine; motor/generator augments shaft power and increases generator output

evidence.snippet: **AXX****P** – Parallel hybrid. Motor/generator can assist the IC engine in providing additional shaft power for time-limited requirements (sprint, take-off, etc.). Additionally, this topology provides more generator output than the ‘N’ configuration.

products.notes: Also labeled A99HF in the site footer (resource-0002 line 56); the main product listing uses A99H.

products.name: A99S
products.descriptor: Series hybrid A99 variant converting all engine shaft power to electrical energy for gen-set or electric propulsion use

evidence.snippet: **AXX****S** – Full series hybrid whereby all available engine shaft power is converted to electrical energy. Use cases include electrically driven craft and gen-sets.

unresolved_questions: All individual product detail pages (/a33n, /a33hf, /a33-engine, /a99-engine, /a99h, /a99s) were skipped_class_budget and not fetched — specs, displacement, power output, weight, and any NDAA/compliance claims are unknown.
A33S lacks a dedicated hyperlink on the platform-strategy page (unlike A33N and A33HF), so its production readiness is uncertain.
A99H is labeled both 'A99H' (main product listing line 45) and 'A99HF' (footer line 56) — exact commercial name unclear without fetching the product page.
No NDAA Section 848/889 or Blue UAS compliance claims found anywhere in the fetched corpus.
